I’m locating the `21-4-extract-workflow-runner` implementation and reading the story artifacts, diff, and relevant tests first. Then I’ll check for behavioral regressions, security/architecture violations, and whether the implementation actually covers its intended acceptance criteria.I’ve got the story spec and the touched runner files. Next I’m comparing `workflow-machine.ts` against the new `workflow-runner.ts`, then I’ll run the narrow verification gates that matter for this extraction: boundaries, targeted workflow tests, and the story’s structural checks.The extraction itself looks behavior-preserving so far, but I’m checking for the gaps that actually decide pass/fail: repository policy enforcement, the story’s structural gates, and whether the runner still satisfies the existing workflow tests through the shim.The boundary failure is confirmed and reproducible. I’m still waiting on the broader targeted workflow tests to separate “policy-only failure” from any behavioral regressions introduced by the extraction.The runner passes the targeted workflow/health/run tests, so I’m not seeing a functional regression there. I’m finishing the AC audit now with build/typecheck and the story-required artifacts, because those can still make the story fail even when behavior is intact.1. [src/lib/workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L19) introduces four bare `catch {}` blocks at lines 19, 22, 39, and 42 without the required `// IGNORE:` annotation. That violates enforced repository policy `NFR3`, and the failure is reproducible in [src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts#L51). This is an architecture violation and it makes the story fail the regression gate.

2. AC coverage is incomplete for Story 21-4 because AC2 requires the full Vitest suite to pass with zero failures, and that cannot be true while the boundary suite is failing. The targeted behavior checks are otherwise healthy, but the implementation still does not satisfy the story as written. The story-level verification artifacts are also missing: `verification/21-4-extract-workflow-runner-proof.md` and `docs/exec-plans/active/21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md` were not present.

No functional or security regression stood out beyond that. `npm run build` passed, `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` passed, `workflow-runner.ts` is within the 150-line budget at 149 lines, and targeted `vitest -t 'health|workflow|run'` passed (`491` tests).

<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. Bare `catch {}` blocks without `// IGNORE:` in [src/lib/workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L19), [src/lib/workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L22), [src/lib/workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L39), and [src/lib/workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L42). This violates enforced NFR3 in [src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/__tests__/boundaries.test.ts#L51) and causes the suite to fail.

2. Story 21-4 AC coverage is not complete because AC2 cannot pass while the boundary test fails, and the required story artifacts are missing: `verification/21-4-extract-workflow-runner-proof.md` and `docs/exec-plans/active/21-4-extract-workflow-runner.md`.
</issues>
